title: Coelogyne macgregorii
taxonomy: Coelogyne macgregorii is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is native to the Philippines. This species is classified within the genus Coelogyne and is further grouped into the subtribe Coelogyninae. The subtribe Coelogyninae is part of the tribe Epidendroideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse groups of orchids. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of Coelogyne macgregorii with other orchid species.

intro: Coelogyne macgregorii is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of the Philippines, characterized by its showy white and yellow flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne macgregorii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coelogyne macgregorii is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, often used to treat respiratory infections and as a natural expectorant to aid in the removal of mucus and phlegm.
